title: She’s running her fifth Boston Marathon in support of Dana-Farber
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Amanda Bathory, 45, from Auburndale, is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Bathory started running the Boston Marathon after losing her mother to cancer, using it as a way to support cancer research.
Her participation in the race symbolizes a recommitment to the cause, carrying memories and hope for cancer patients and families.

title: A rat race to the rescue: MSPCA-Angell seeks homes after 163 rodents surrendered
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MSPCA-Angell seeks homes for 163 rats surrendered in Essex County, the largest such surrender in five years.
The organization has kept 53 rats for adoption, working with shelters across Massachusetts and New Hampshire for the others.
Rats, known for intelligence and social nature, are available across MSPCA's locations, aiming to find them suitable homes.

title: ‘Nobody wants to see this’: Knicks fan Rich Eisen’s rant about the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rich Eisen expressed surprise at the Celtics' strong performance despite roster changes and Jayson Tatum's injury.
The Celtics defeated the Bucks 108-81 without Jaylen Brown, showcasing depth with players like Payton Pritchard and Derrick White.
Eisen noted the Celtics' rise is concerning to other teams and that his son, a Boston fan, has divergent sports loyalties.

title: Boston 25 News reporter Drew Karedes leaves after nine years
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Drew Karedes leaves Boston 25 News after nine years, announced his departure on social media.
Karedes started at Boston 25 News in March 2017 as a general assignment reporter.
His reporting covered diverse topics, including ICE arrests and community issues in Boston and Lynn.

title: 12 vehicles involved in series of crashes on I-95 ramp in Danvers
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Twelve vehicles were involved in three separate crashes Wednesday on an I-95 ramp in Danvers, state police reported.
Icy conditions on the ramp to Route 1 North at Exit 70 caused the multi-vehicle collisions, according to Massachusetts State Police.
Three adults sustained back injuries, and all involved were taken to local hospitals for evaluation after the crash.

title: 2 people, including child, hit by car in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An adult and a child were hit by a car in Dorchester, Boston, on Tuesday night.
The incident occurred around 7 p.m. at the intersection of Mora and Washington streets.
Both victims sustained minor injuries and were taken to a hospital; the driver stayed at the scene.

title: Auburn police seek missing Mass. woman last seen in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Auburn Police seek help locating 47-year-old Jennifer Remillard, last seen January 31 in Sarasota, Florida.
Jennifer Remillard is described as 5'9", 130 pounds, with blonde hair, and driving a gray 2012 Toyota Camry.
Public can contact Auburn Police at 508-832-7777 or email [email protected] with information on her whereabouts.

title: Boston’s innovation economy hits a new milestone in global rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Five Boston-area firms made the LexisNexis 'Top 100 Global Innovators' list, marking a record for the region.
Boston ties with L.A. for third in U.S. for total companies on list, behind Silicon Valley and New York City.
Companies include Alnylam Pharmaceuticals, Moderna, Flagship Pioneering, Orna Therapeutics, and SharkNinja, illustrating Boston's innovation strength.

title: Is Scituate native Conor Garland a worthwhile trade target for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Conor Garland, from Scituate, is signed with the Canucks through 2032 at a $6 million cap hit.
Garland, a middle-six winger, averages 18.8 goals and 48.8 points over the last four seasons.
The Bruins are considering Garland as a trade target, but his fit with their current timeline is uncertain.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 4)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington colonial at 1210 Washington St. sold for $500,000; features 2 bedrooms on a 14,792-square-foot lot.
Acton's 7 Concetta Circle, built in 1988, and over 2,726 sq ft, sold for $1,175,000 with 4 bedrooms.
Boston condo at 100 Belvidere St. #8H, 1,692 sq ft with 2 bedrooms, sold for $3,700,000.
